raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased a stove from Best Buy, online. Product # SKU : XXXX and model Model : XXXX. 

The website has the stove listed for {$1100.00}. 

The add states you will save {$420.00} by buying from best buy, instead of purchasing from a competitor for {$1600.00}. 

Additionally the webpage offers the purchase through the best buy credit card for 18 months at 0 % interest ; if you do the math on the monthly payments x months, this also equals {$1100.00}. I applied for this, was approved, and completed the purchase using the Best Buy Credit Card. 

I then received a statement with my Citibank login information, only to log in and find that Best Buy charged me {$1600.00} for the stove. I can assure you, no where during the checkout was I told, nor agreed to this amount. 

I have screenshots of the product webpage that saved, and the product in my cart, which backs up my statements made above. I have messaged citibank only to receive delayed responses saying they are reviewing my case and will mail me a letter with resolution, but I must submit further documentation via email or fax ; However, they do not provide a working email or fax number to submit said documentation. 

Any normal business wouldn't take multiple weeks to resolve. This is shady business practice and I will not pay them a dime more than what was advertised, and my GOOD CREDIT history should not have to be negatively affected in doing so. The balance should be corrected, last I understood false advertisement in the USA is ILLEGAL.
